records.ety: [L. repudiatus, p. p. of repudiare to repudiate, reject, fr. repudium separation, divorce; pref. re- re- + pudere to be ashamed.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Repudiated (-?`t?d); p. pr. & vb. n. Repudiating.]

defs: To cast off; to disavow; to have nothing to do with; to renounce; to reject.
quotes: Servitude is to be repudiated with greater care.

defs: To divorce, put away, or discard, as a wife, or a woman one has promised to marry.
quotes: His separation from Terentis, whom he repudiated not long afterward.

defs: To refuse to acknowledge or to pay; to disclaim; as, the State has repudiated its debts.
